mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects economic expansion that drives corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward 60-trading-day returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during GDP growth periods are not significantly above baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above the 4.5% threshold, it signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If more than 50% of triggered instances result in positive 20-day forward returns, or if average return after trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains at or above 4.5% while housing starts fall to or below 1.4 million units, the tight monetary policy combined with housing market contraction creates a headwind for economic growth, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
regime_hint: tight monetary policy with housing weakness
failure_condition: If triggered events produce a non-negative median 20-day forward return, or if the proportion of negative returns is less than 60% over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary regime defined by unemployment above 6%, increases in industrial production indicate supply-side recovery that boosts corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6%, positive INDPRO growth leads to positive 60-day returns less than 60% of the time, or average return does not exceed the unconditional mean during that regime.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 4.0% signals the start of an easing cycle that lowers discount rates, driving a short-term positive reaction in S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: If average 5-day forward return after trigger is not positive and statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05), or if the proportion of positive returns is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales grow month-over-month by more than 0.5% but payroll employment simultaneously declines, it indicates a divergence between consumer spending and labor market health that warns of future weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If triggered events produce a negative 20-day forward return less than 55% of the time, or if the median return is not significantly negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in real retail sales reflects robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth does not lead to significantly positive 20-day returns in a majority of rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a severe contraction in residential investment, which historically precedes economic downturns and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the proportion of negative 60-day forward returns after trigger is less than 60%, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over the full sample.
